summary: topology compatibility_error not recovered
description:
The problem is topology compatibility_error field is not updated when the problematic state of the connection is recovered.

Reproduce:
 * Make the client connection
 * Stop mongodb and start after 10 - 15 seconds
 * The client connection is already marked the compatibility_error.
 * No more communication with mongodb until the restart the rust application.

Error Output:
{code:java}
Error { kind: ServerSelectionError { message: "Server at 127.0.0.1:27017 reports wire version 0, but this version of the MongoDB Rust driver requires at least 6 (MongoDB 3.6)." } }
{code}
Expected Behavior:

The connection should be recoverable.

summary: README.md needs to include Bson in `use`. example in README.md fails otherwise
description:
_{color:#0747a6}here: [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver#inserting-documents-into-a-collection]{color}_

 

_{color:#0747a6}currently is:{color}_
h3. Finding documents in a collection

use bson::\{doc, bson};

 
 _{color:#0747a6}should be:{color}_
  
h3. Finding documents in a collection

use bson::\{doc, bson, Bson};

summary: tokio panics when tasks are spawned after runtime has been dropped
description: If {{AsyncRuntime::execute }}is called after the runtime has been dropped, it causes tokio to panic internally. We should check if the runtime is still alive before attempting to spawn these tasks.

summary: Cannot restore BinarySubtype
Disable default features on pbkdf2 crate
description:
The pbkdf2 crate pulls in an old version of the rand crate (0.5.x), but this dependency can be removed if pbkdf2's default features aren't used.

I checked the code, and indeed default features can be disabled. Doing this drops 7 crates (rand 0.5.x, rand_core 0.3.x, rand_core 0.4.x, etc).

summary: Support configuring the DNS Resolver from something else than `Default::default()`
description: Right now the `SrvResolver` is newed will the default configuration: [(srv.rs line 24)|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/blob/2dabb7aa6d7a14eabb1b2e1749176a22e8448b6d/src/srv.rs#L24], this means that all domain name resolution is done by google's DNS servers, and restrict name resolution to "public" domain name. I propose that the Resolver can be configured to use the `Resolver::from_system_conf()` method allowing custom DNS server to be used (this would be very useful in kubernetes)

summary: Replace `futures_timer` with runtime-specific delays
description: We've been having some spurious failures in our CI due to {{futures_timer}}, and using TSAN with a simple {{futures_timer}} example gives some warnings, so we should switch from using {{futures_timer}} and implement an agnostic {{delay_for}} function in our runtime module.

summary: Remove constants for subtypes and element types
description: As part of the work to stabilize the BSON API, we should consider removing the constants defined for binary subtypes and element types. Considering that we provide enums for each of these already with `From` implementations in both directions to convert to and from {{u8}}, we can cut down on the API surface of the [spec|https://docs.rs/bson/0.14.1/bson/spec/index.html] module quite a bit by removing them.

summary: Remove deprecated json methods from Bson
description: {{Bson::to_json}}, {{Bson::into_json}}, and {{Bson::from_json}} were all deprecated many versions ago after being replaced by {{From}} implementations in both directions for {{Bson}} and {{serde_json::Value}}. Rather than codify methods that we don't intend for users to ever use as part of our stable API, we should remove these before stabilization.

summary: Remove Bson::as_*_mut helpers for types that are Copy
description: {{Bson}} provides mutable getters to for each of the variants in the enum, e.g. {{Bson::as_document_mut}}, which returns an {{Option<&mut Document}}. While these are useful for most types, they don't provide much utility for types that implement Copy (e.g. numeric types and bool), as none of the functionality on those types is implemented in methods that take references to {{self}}. To cut down on unnecessary API surface, we should remove those extraneous {{as_mut}} helpers.

summary: Remove ObjectId::with_timestamp
description: {{ObjectId}} defines a [{{with_timestamp}}|https://docs.rs/bson/0.14.1/bson/oid/struct.ObjectId.html#method.with_timestamp] constructor to create an object id with a given timestamp with the rest of the bytes unspecified, with the ostensible purpose of allowing people to query for object ids within a given timestamp range. We should decide if we in fact do want to provide this sort of functionality, and if not, remove it.

summary: Remove Document::insert_bson
description: Since {{Document::insert}} is generic over {{Into<String>}} and {{Into<Bson>}}, we can remove the {{Document::insert_bson}} method, as all types implement {{Into}} themselves.

summary: Convert variants of the BSON enum to use structs instead of multiple fields
description: Currently, we represent many of the BSON types as enum variants with multiple fields; for example, binary values are represented as {{Bson::Binary(BinarySubtype, Vec<u8>)}}. These multi-field enum variants are a bit unwieldy, as they make it tough for users to define a struct field of an exact BSON type, as they lack a concrete type that they map to. To make them easier to use, we should consider replacing all multi-field enum variants with a single field of a concrete struct type defined specifically for that type. This also will allow us to define type-specific functionality on the subtypes, which could potentially be useful.

summary: Remove timestamp/counter getters on ObjectId
description: {{ObjectId}} currently provides two helpers, [https://docs.rs/bson/0.14.1/bson/oid/struct.ObjectId.html#method.counter|counter] and [{{timestamp}}|https://docs.rs/bson/0.14.1/bson/oid/struct.ObjectId.html#method.timestamp] to get specific parts of the ObjectId. We should decide if we in fact want to provide this functionality, and if not, remove them.

summary: Remove oid::Error::HostnameError
description: Since we no longer use the hostname as a way of generating the system identifier, we can remove this error variant, as we don't ever return it anymore.

summary: Connection string uri password encoding
description:
I am constructing a mongo client from `ClientOptions parse` and giving it a connection string uri that works in the Mongo Go driver version 1.3.1.  However, the Rust driver is failing with an error "password must be URL encoded".  The password does contain a "#" symbol but the Go driver does not complain.  A password with no special characters or an "!" symbol work in both the Go and Rust driver.  

My expectation is that both the Go and Rust driver handle connection strings the same.

summary: TailableAwait option doesn't seem to work in async driver
Add 'allowDiskUse' option to find command
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    Update find() API in drivers to add allowDiskUse support.
    See DRIVERS-694 for updated details.

summary: Support for 'authorizedDatabases' option
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    {panel:title=Useful Info|borderStyle=dashed|borderColor=#ccc|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2} 
h4.Summary
Add support for a new parameter `authorizedDatabases` to database enumeration spec.

*Lead*: Jeff
*Author*: Robert
*POCs*:

*Relevant Spec Changes*: 

{panel}
    See DRIVERS-685 for updated details.

summary: Improve invalid URI option error message
description:
Right now, whenever a user specifies an invalid option, the driver emits the following error:
{code}ArgumentError { message: "invalid option warning" } }
{code}
The message has a few issues:
 * It doesn't specify which option is invalid
 * It says "warning" even though it's an error and will prevent URI parsing.

We should update the message to address the previous two points. As an added bonus, we could consider using a [string similarity|https://docs.rs/strsim/0.10.0/strsim/] lookup on the list of valid options to suggest alternatives if a user made a typo, similar to how the Rust compiler tries to give suggestions for typos.

summary: $readPreference not being set against some topology types
description: Commands sent to non-sharded/non-single topologies do not have their {{$readPreference}} set. This can lead to "non master" errors when using non-primary read preferences.

summary: Update multi-threaded examples to use tasks
description: There are a number of examples demonstrating that {{Client}} / {{Database}} / {{Collection}} are safe to use across threads. Now that the driver is becoming async-focused, we should try to update these examples to use tasks instead. One concern is figuring out how to do this in an async-runtime agnostic way, or at least a way that compiles regardless of the feature flags defined.

summary: Sync API not included in the generated documentation
description: Due to the sync API not being enabled by default, it isn't included in the documentation. We should update our features set to allow for this like [tokio|https://docs.rs/tokio/0.2.20/tokio/#modules] does.

summary: Remove old doc! macro syntax
description: When the BSON library's {{doc!}} macro was first written, the "{{:}}" character was not allowed in macro patterns, so the syntax for a key-value pair was implemented as {{"key" => value}} rather than {{"key": value}}. Later, when Rust added support for using "{{:}}" in macro patterns, the old syntax for {{doc!}} was kept around for backwards compatibility reasons. Since it's been a couple of years since that change was made, we should consider finally getting rid of the legacy syntax before 1.0.

summary: Consider renaming OrderedDocument to Document
description: In the bson library, we have a public type called {{OrderedDocument}} which {{Document}} is type aliased to. We should consider renaming this type to just {{Document}} and remove the type alias, since it implies the existence of an {{UnorderedDocument}} which doesn't in fact exist. If we add an unordered document in the future, we can still name it as such.

summary: Rewrite ReadConcern as a struct
description:
Currently, {{ReadConcern}} is implemented as an enum. The spec explicitly defines it as an object with a single {{level}} field, however. This might be so that future fields could be added to {{ReadConcern}} in addition to {{level}}, similar to how {{WriteConcern}} has several fields.

As implemented, adding any new fields would be a breaking change to the driver's enum based implementation, but if it were implemented as a struct, doing so would not be.

summary: Label BSON error enum cases
description:
Currently, each of the BSON library's error's enum cases' associated values are unlabeled, which makes it hard to determine what the values mean without looking at the {{Display}} implementation or reading the docstrings. In the driver, we label all associated values unless they're just being wrapped directly. We should consider a similar convention for the BSON library.

This would be a breaking change, so it would be need to done before 1.0.

summary: Add documentation for BSON library error variants
description: The variants of the various error enums in the BSON library are undocumented. We should document them so users can meaningfully handle them.

summary: BSON deserializer always ignores unknown fields
Replace unneeded serde enum cases in DecoderError
description: {{DecoderError}} currently has enum cases for each of the provided methods on the {{serde::de::Error}} trait ([https://docs.rs/serde/1.0.110/serde/de/trait.Error.html]), but these are unnecessary. The serde docs themselves state that "Most deserializers should only need to provide the {{Error::custom}} method and inherit the default behavior for the other methods.".

summary: Remove implementation of `Error::description` in BSON library
description: Rust 1.42.0 deprecated the {{description}} method of the {{Error}} trait. We should remove our custom implementations of it in the BSON library.

summary: Update extended JSON format
description: The BSON corpus tests aren't currently implemented because the bson library doesn't support the modern extended JSON format from the specs. We should update the supported extJSON format and start running the BSON corpus tests.

summary: Rename "Tag" Acknowledgement case to "Custom"
description:
The official docs have settled on the term "custom write concern name" for the non-majority string value for {{w}} in a WriteConcern. We should update our field name to reflect that, as "write tag" isn't a commonly understood term.

see: [https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/write-concern/#writeconcern.%3Ccustom-write-concern-name%3E]

summary: Return errors when unacknowledged write concerns are passed in
description: Since we're not implementing unacknowledged writes before 1.0, we should return informative errors when users pass in unacknowledged write concerns

summary: allowDiskUse option for find should be documented as only being supported in 4.4+
description: See DRIVERS-1193 for updated details.

summary: Standardize on options exporting strategy
description:
Currently, the authentication options ( {{Credential}} and {{AuthMechanism}} ) are exported from the public {{options::auth}} module. All other options in the driver are simply exported from the {{options}} module itself.

We should export everything at the top level {{options}} module.

summary: Move encode_document and decode_document into the Document namespace
description:
The BSON library currently offers free-floating public {{encode_document}} and {{decode_document}} functions for converting {{Document}} to/from bytes. These were defined as free floating in an attempt to match how {{serde_json}} defines [its methods|https://docs.rs/serde_json/1.0.53/serde_json/#functions], but those were defined as such because they're generic over the type being encoded/decoded. {{encode_document}} and {{decode_document}} operate specifically with {{Document}}, so they would make more sense as methods in the {{Document}} namespace, e.g. {{Document::decode}} and {{document.encode()}}.

Additionally, if RUST-428 is implemented, these free-floating functions will become redundant if left as-is. As methods on {{Document}}, they will simply become nice conveniences.

As this would be a breaking change, it must be done pre 1.0.

summary: Add more detailed examples to BSON library documentation
description:
The "basic usage" examples included in the BSON library's documentation omit any examples using serde or the {{doc!}} macro, both of which important and common usages of the library. The section should be updated to include such examples.

The docs also do not mention MongoDB in any capacity, and only include a small snippet about what BSON is. This could be updated to provide more context and direct people towards to driver's crate.

summary: Improve naming of Bson and ElementType cases
description:
The enum cases for [{{Bson}}|https://docs.rs/bson/0.14.1/bson/enum.Bson.html] and [{{ElementType}}|https://docs.rs/bson/0.14.1/bson/spec/enum.ElementType.html] are named a bit inconsistently, both with each other and with other drivers. We should consider renaming these to be more consistent in both senses.

For some suggestions on name changes, see [this discussion|https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/pull/152#discussion_r404373313].

summary: bson-rust is missing binary subtype 6
description: The bson-rust library is missing the new binary subtype 6. It needs to be added to the existing enums and From traits.

summary: Fix Display implementation for Bson Binary type
Standardize API with other serde libraries
description:
{{serde}} based libraries have a somewhat stable layout of their APIs. The BSON library conforms to most of these ideas, but differs in some ways. We should strive to be as consistent with the other popular {{serde}} libraries to make using the BSON library natural for users of other popular libraries.

Here are some ways the BSON library differs: 

*Encode/Decode vs Serialize/Deserialize* 

The BSON library currently defines an {{Encoder}} and a {{Decoder}}, and uses verbs like {{decode}} and {{encode}}. In Rust, the verbs "serialize" and "deserialize" are more commonly used however (hence {{serde}}). We should rename our types to fit this pattern.

*Namespacing*

The BSON library currently uses prefixes for distinguishing between serialization types and deserialization types (e.g. {{DecoderResult}} and {{EncoderError}}). The other popular serde libraries (serde_json, toml, serde-xml-rs) split these into separate public modules {{ser}} and {{de}}. This helps to declutter the top-level namespace and organize types logically.

-*Value Type Name*-

-Each of the other popular serde libraries (serde_json, toml, serde_yaml, serde-xml-rs) defines a top level type {{Value}} to refer to their value type (e.g. {{serde_json::Value}}). The BSON library calls it {{Bson}}. We should consider renaming this to {{Value}} to align with the other libraries. It can be disambiguated from them via the crate name (e.g. {{bson::Value}}).-

summary: Enable and configure TCP Keepalive by default
description: See DRIVERS-383 for details.

summary: Reduce default keepalive time to align with Azure defaults
description: See DRIVERS-1228 for updated details.

summary: Allow hinting the delete command
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    
    {panel:title=Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    This change adds a new hint parameter to each statement of the delete command, which works just like hinting in update. In addition, a hint parameter can now be used in findAndModify with remove=true, as well.
    {panel}
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    We recently added hint support for updates; we should do the same for the delete command. This work should also lift the restriction that the {{findAndModify}} command cannot include a hint if its {{remove}} parameter is set.
    
    See DRIVERS-908 for updated details.

summary: support ability to pass hint to update
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    New CRUD spec tests in https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/60adafcffa07174ac54c6e8658bd37a3faf350c3
    See DRIVERS-751 for updated details.

summary: Improve deserialization error messages
description:
(tracking ticket for https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/issues/84)

Currently, when a deserialization error occurs, the error returned by the BSON library identifies the type that was unable to be deserialized, but not the name of the field. This can be ambiguous if there are multiple fields of the same type that are being deserialized. We should investigate how we can improve these error messages, potentially by adding the name of the field and the value that was unable to be deserialized.

summary: Ensure that the WriteConcernError "errInfo" object is propagated
Add a bson corpus test with invalid type for $code when $scope is also present
description: See DRIVERS-1326 for updated details.

summary: Lift restriction on authSource without credentials
description: See DRIVERS-796 for updated details.
